questions.question_text: A car travels 100 m in 10 s at constant speed. What is its speed?
questions.markscheme_answer: Speed = distance / time = 100 m / 10 s = 10 m/s

questions.question_text: A ball is dropped from rest and falls for 3 s. How far does it fall? (g = 10 m/s²)
A car accelerates from 5 m/s to 25 m/s over a distance of 60 m. What is its acceleration?
A projectile is launched at 30° above horizontal with initial speed 20 m/s. Find the maximum height reached. (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.markscheme_answer: v_y = 20 sin(30°) = 10 m/s. At max height, v = 0: h = v²/(2g) = 100/20 = 5 m

questions.question_text: Two balls are thrown simultaneously: Ball A is thrown upward at 15 m/s from ground level, Ball B is dropped from 25 m height. When and where do they meet? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.markscheme_answer: Position A: y_A = 15t - 5t². Position B: y_B = 25 - 5t². When they meet: 15t - 5t² = 25 - 5t² → 15t = 25 → t = 5/3 s. Height: y = 15(5/3) - 5(25/9) = 25 - 125/9 = 100/9 ≈ 11.1 m

questions.question_text: A 2 kg object experiences a net force of 10 N. What is its acceleration?
A 5 kg box rests on a horizontal surface. The coefficient of static friction is 0.4. What is the maximum horizontal force that can be applied before the box moves? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 3 kg object moving at 4 m/s collides with a stationary 1 kg object. They stick together. Find their common velocity after collision.
questions.markscheme_answer: Conservation of momentum: m₁v₁ + m₂v₂ = (m₁+m₂)v. 3×4 + 0 = 4v → v = 3 m/s

questions.question_text: A 2 kg block on a 30° incline is connected by a massless rope over a pulley to a hanging 3 kg mass. Find the acceleration of the system. (μ = 0.2, g = 10 m/s²)
questions.markscheme_answer: For hanging mass: 30 - T = 3a. For block: T - 2g(sin30° + μcos30°) = 2a. T - 10 - 0.2×2×10×0.866 = 2a. T - 13.46 = 2a. Solving: a = (30 - 13.46)/5 = 3.3 m/s²

questions.question_text: A rocket of initial mass 1000 kg ejects gas at 500 m/s relative to the rocket at a rate of 5 kg/s. What is the initial acceleration of the rocket if it launches vertically? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.markscheme_answer: Thrust = v_exhaust × dm/dt = 500 × 5 = 2500 N. Weight = 1000 × 10 = 10000 N. Net force = 2500 - 10000 = -7500 N (cannot lift off initially). If thrust exceeds weight: a = (F - mg)/m

questions.question_text: A 10 N force moves an object 5 m in the direction of the force. Calculate the work done.
A 2 kg ball is dropped from 20 m. What is its speed just before hitting the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 1500 kg car accelerates from rest to 20 m/s in 10 s. Calculate the average power developed.
A spring with k = 200 N/m is compressed 0.3 m. A 0.5 kg ball is placed against it and released. The ball then slides up a frictionless ramp. What maximum height does it reach?
questions.markscheme_answer: Spring PE = ½kx² = ½×200×0.09 = 9 J. At max height: mgh = 9 → h = 9/(0.5×10) = 1.8 m

questions.question_text: A roller coaster car of mass 500 kg starts from rest at height 40 m. It loses 20% of its energy to friction by the time it reaches a loop of radius 10 m. Find the normal force on the car at the top of the loop.
What type of heat transfer occurs primarily through electromagnetic waves?
How much heat is needed to raise the temperature of 2 kg of water by 10°C? (c = 4200 J/kg·K)
A 0.5 kg block of copper at 100°C is placed in 2 kg of water at 20°C. Find the final equilibrium temperature. (c_copper = 400 J/kg·K, c_water = 4200 J/kg·K)
A metal rod of length 1 m and cross-sectional area 0.01 m² connects two reservoirs at 100°C and 0°C. If k = 50 W/m·K, calculate the rate of heat transfer.
A black body sphere of radius 0.1 m and emissivity 0.9 is at 500 K in surroundings at 300 K. Calculate the net power radiated. (σ = 5.67×10⁻⁸ W/m²·K⁴)
What is the relationship between wave speed (v), frequency (f), and wavelength (λ)?
A wave has frequency 500 Hz and wavelength 0.68 m. Calculate the wave speed.
A transverse wave on a string has amplitude 5 cm and period 0.2 s. The string is 4 m long with 3 complete wavelengths. Find the wave speed.
Two coherent sources emit waves of wavelength 2 cm. A point P is 30 cm from one source and 34 cm from the other. Is there constructive or destructive interference at P?
questions.markscheme_answer: Path difference = 34 - 30 = 4 cm = 2λ. Since path difference = nλ (n = 2), there is constructive interference.

questions.question_text: Light of wavelength 600 nm passes through a double slit with separation 0.1 mm. The screen is 2 m away. Calculate the distance between the central maximum and the third bright fringe.
What are the points of zero displacement in a standing wave called?
A string fixed at both ends has length 1.2 m. What is the wavelength of the third harmonic?
questions.markscheme_answer: For nth harmonic: L = nλ/2. For n=3: λ = 2L/3 = 2.4/3 = 0.8 m

questions.question_text: A pipe open at both ends has length 0.85 m. The speed of sound is 340 m/s. What is the fundamental frequency?
questions.markscheme_answer: For open pipe: f₁ = v/(2L) = 340/(2×0.85) = 200 Hz

questions.question_text: A pipe closed at one end resonates at 170 Hz in its fundamental mode. What is the frequency of the next resonant mode? (Speed of sound = 340 m/s)
questions.markscheme_answer: Closed pipe: only odd harmonics. Next mode is 3rd harmonic: f₃ = 3f₁ = 3 × 170 = 510 Hz

questions.question_text: Two tuning forks of frequencies 256 Hz and 260 Hz produce beats. A string resonates with the beat frequency. If the string has mass per unit length 0.01 kg/m and length 0.4 m, what tension produces resonance in the fundamental mode?
State Ohm's Law.
questions.markscheme_answer: V = IR (Voltage equals current times resistance)

questions.question_text: A 12 V battery is connected to a 4 Ω resistor. Calculate the current flowing.
Three resistors of 2 Ω, 3 Ω, and 6 Ω are connected in parallel. Find the equivalent resistance.
A cell of emf 6 V and internal resistance 0.5 Ω is connected to an external resistance of 2.5 Ω. Find the terminal voltage and power dissipated in the external resistor.
questions.markscheme_answer: I = E/(R+r) = 6/3 = 2 A. Terminal voltage V = E - Ir = 6 - 1 = 5 V. Power = I²R = 4 × 2.5 = 10 W

questions.question_text: In a Wheatstone bridge, R₁ = 100 Ω, R₂ = 200 Ω, R₃ = 150 Ω. What value of R₄ balances the bridge? If the galvanometer has resistance 50 Ω and the bridge is supplied by 6 V, find the galvanometer current when R₄ = 250 Ω.
questions.markscheme_answer: For balance: R₁/R₂ = R₃/R₄ → 100/200 = 150/R₄ → R₄ = 300 Ω. When R₄ = 250 Ω, bridge is unbalanced. Use Thevenin equivalent or mesh analysis to find galvanometer current.
